dependencies: Third-party suppliers including certain single-sourced suppliers for various components
Global supply chain for key production inputs including steel, aluminum, copper, rubber, chemicals, polymers
Reliance on third-party suppliers for raw materials and key inputs with no long-term pricing contracts
risks: Supply chain disruptions including component, raw material and chip shortages
Supplier disputes regarding pricing or performance could adversely affect ability to supply products
Shortage or discontinuation of certain raw materials or key inputs
Disruption of supply chain could reduce, prevent or delay production and shipment
Broad-based inflation, escalation of commodities costs, transportation and logistics costs, labor costs

regulatory_risks: Federal Railroad Administration (FRA) regulations and standards
Association of American Railroads (AAR) standards
International Union of Railways (UIC) standards
European Railway Agencies regulations
European Committees for Standardization requirements
Technical Standards of Interoperability in Europe
Environmental laws and regulations governing air emissions, water discharges, hazardous substances
U.S. Environmental Protection Agency emissions and noise standards
European Union emissions and noise standards
Climate change regulation and greenhouse gas emission restrictions
Anti-corruption laws including U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, French Sapin II Law, U.K. Bribery Act
Export control regulations administered by U.S. Department of Commerce Bureau of Industry and Security
Economic sanctions by U.S. Department of Treasury Office of Foreign Assets Control
Customs requirements and currency exchange regulations
Transfer pricing regulations
Product safety, operations, maintenance and mechanical standards
Positive Train Control (PTC) equipment regulations
Certification and qualification requirements for suppliers in various jurisdictions
New product testing and approval processes mandated by government agencies
Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 provisions including book minimum tax and excise tax on stock buybacks

sanction_risks: Sanctions and embargoes related to Russia-Ukraine conflict
Potential retaliatory action by Russian government including nationalization of foreign businesses and assets in Russia
Economic sanctions on countries and persons administered by U.S. Treasury Office of Foreign Assets Control
Supply and distribution impacts in Kazakhstan as indirect result from Russian invasion of Ukraine
Currency impacts in Kazakhstan from ongoing conflict
Regional instability and geopolitical shifts from international conflicts
Strategic decisions to alter transportation and distribution routes due to conflicts
